description: Initialize the Bazel monorepo with bzlmod, scaffold the directory structure defined in ARCHITECTURE.md, and create the first sub-repo: a Mermaid C4 diagram tool for rendering the platform's architecture diagrams.

userStories.title: Initialize MODULE.bazel and Bazel configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Bazel build system initialized with bzlmod so that the monorepo has a working build foundation. Create MODULE.bazel with module name and initial bazel_dep entries (rules_python, rules_nodejs). Create .bazelrc with common settings. Create .bazelversion pinning Bazel 7 LTS. Create root BUILD.bazel.
acceptanceCriteria: MODULE.bazel exists with module name and bazel_dep entries for rules_python and rules_nodejs
.bazelrc exists with common build settings
.bazelversion exists pinning Bazel 7 LTS
Root BUILD.bazel exists
bazel build //... succeeds with no errors
userStories.notes: Effort: 4:00. Priority: p0. Foundation task — everything else depends on this. COMPLETED: MODULE.bazel with rules_python 1.1.0 and aspect_rules_js 2.1.2, .bazelrc, .bazelversion 7.6.1, root BUILD.bazel.

userStories.title: Scaffold monorepo directory structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the standard monorepo directory structure in place so that services, libraries, and tools have a defined home. Create all directories defined in ARCHITECTURE.md (services, libs, schemas, sdks, workflows, pipelines, infra, deploy, tools, scripts, third_party). Each gets an empty BUILD.bazel and a brief README.md.
acceptanceCriteria: All directories from ARCHITECTURE.md exist under monorepo/: services, libs, schemas, sdks, workflows, pipelines, infra, deploy, tools, scripts, third_party
Each directory contains a BUILD.bazel file
Each directory contains a README.md explaining its purpose
bazel build //... still succeeds
userStories.notes: Effort: 1:00. Priority: p0. Depends on US-001. COMPLETED: All 11 directories scaffolded with BUILD.bazel and README.md.

userStories.title: Set up architecture-diagram tool with Mermaid CLI
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Mermaid-based diagram rendering tool so that architecture diagrams can be generated from source files via Bazel. Create tools/architecture-diagram/ with package.json (mermaid-cli, elk dependency), mermaidrc.json (ELK layout, theme config), BUILD.bazel with render targets, and README.md.
acceptanceCriteria: tools/architecture-diagram/package.json exists with @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli and ELK dependency
tools/architecture-diagram/mermaidrc.json configures ELK layout engine and consistent theme
tools/architecture-diagram/BUILD.bazel defines render targets
tools/architecture-diagram/README.md documents usage
bazel build //tools/architecture-diagram/... succeeds and produces SVG/PNG output
userStories.notes: Effort: 4:00. Priority: p0. Depends on US-002. COMPLETED: Full Mermaid pipeline with rules_js, ELK layout, sample diagram renders to SVG via bazel build.

userStories.title: Create CI wrapper scripts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want convenience scripts for common CI operations so that builds, tests, and diagram rendering can be run with simple commands. Create scripts/build.sh, scripts/test.sh, and scripts/render-diagrams.sh. Each wraps the corresponding Bazel command. All must be runnable locally. Scripts should accept an optional Bazel target pattern for scoped execution.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/build.sh wraps bazel build and accepts optional target pattern
scripts/test.sh wraps bazel test and accepts optional target pattern
scripts/render-diagrams.sh wraps the diagram Bazel target
All scripts are executable (chmod +x)
All scripts work locally with no special environment beyond Bazel
userStories.notes: Effort: 1:00. Priority: p1. Depends on US-002. COMPLETED: build.sh, test.sh, render-diagrams.sh created.

userStories.title: Implement affected-targets detection script
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a script that computes affected Bazel targets from the git diff so that CI only builds and tests what changed. Create scripts/affected.sh using bazel query rdeps(//..., set(...)) to walk the dependency graph. Output should be consumable by build.sh and test.sh. Must work locally (diffing against main) and in CI (diffing against the PR base).
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/affected.sh exists and is executable
Script computes affected targets from git diff against base branch
Uses bazel query rdeps(//..., set(...)) for dependency graph traversal
Output is consumable by build.sh and test.sh
Works locally (diffing against main) and in CI (diffing against PR base)
Follows scoping rules from the design doc (e.g., scripts/ changes trigger full CI run)
userStories.notes: Effort: 4:00. Priority: p0. Depends on US-004. COMPLETED: affected.sh with scoping rules, rdeps query, CI_BASE_BRANCH support.

userStories.title: Create initial C4 Context diagram
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a C4 Context diagram showing the platform at the highest level so that stakeholders can understand the system boundaries and external dependencies. Write diagrams/c4-context.mmd showing the platform, its users, and external systems (RSS feed sources, cloud providers). Use Mermaid C4 syntax with ELK layout.
acceptanceCriteria: tools/architecture-diagram/diagrams/c4-context.mmd exists with valid Mermaid C4 syntax
Diagram shows the platform, users, and external systems (RSS sources, cloud providers)
Diagram renders successfully via the Bazel target producing SVG/PNG
userStories.notes: Effort: 2:00. Priority: p1. Depends on US-003. COMPLETED: c4-context.mmd with platform, users, RSS sources, cloud provider.

userStories.title: Create initial C4 Container diagram
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a C4 Container diagram zooming into the platform internals so that the architecture is documented at the container level. Write diagrams/c4-container.mmd showing: FastAPI services, Temporal orchestration, Dagster pipelines, schema registry, intelligent broker, search, k8s compute, Pulumi infra. Use Mermaid C4 syntax with ELK layout.
acceptanceCriteria: tools/architecture-diagram/diagrams/c4-container.mmd exists with valid Mermaid C4 syntax
Diagram shows FastAPI services, Temporal, Dagster, schema registry, intelligent broker, search, k8s, Pulumi
Diagram renders successfully via the Bazel target producing SVG/PNG
userStories.notes: Effort: 4:00. Priority: p1. Depends on US-003 (diagram tool setup).

userStories.title: Validate end-to-end: build, test, render
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to validate the entire monorepo foundation works end-to-end before building on it. Run the full cycle locally: bazel build //..., bazel test //..., and diagram rendering. Fix any issues discovered.
acceptanceCriteria: bazel build //... succeeds with no errors
bazel test //... succeeds with no errors
Diagram rendering produces valid SVG/PNG for both C4 diagrams
scripts/build.sh, scripts/test.sh, and scripts/render-diagrams.sh all work correctly
scripts/affected.sh correctly identifies affected targets
userStories.notes: Effort: 2:00. Priority: p0. Final validation — depends on all other tasks. Natural human review checkpoint.
